{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Utility mark colors are misread or misapplied — red=electric, yellow=gas, blue=water, green=sewer, orange=comm not correctly identified or marked",
    "Incorrect color identification creates strike risk during excavation and violates CGA/811 uniform color code",
    "Marking is non-compliant with APWA/CGA uniform color code standard at rough-complete stage"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately halt excavation activity in affected area until marks are verified and corrected",
    "Re-verify all utility marks against 811 ticket and correct color coding per APWA/CGA uniform color code",
    "Re-train field crew on APWA uniform color code: red=electric, yellow=gas, blue=water, green=sewer, orange=communications",
    "Request re-mark from 811 if original marks are faded or unclear",
    "Document corrected marks and notify inspector for re-inspection before proceeding"
  ],
  "rationale": "Misreading or misapplying utility mark colors is a direct violation of the APWA/CGA uniform color code, which is the industry standard referenced by 811 best practices. At rough-complete stage, incorrect color identification is a critical safety hazard that can lead to utility strikes during excavation. The work is non-compliant and must be corrected before any further excavation or backfill proceeds.",
  "references": [
    "APWA/CGA Uniform Color Code (red=electric, yellow=gas, blue=water, green=sewer, orange=communications)",
    "811/CGA Best Practices for utility locating and marking",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1926.651 - Excavations - utility verification requirements"
  ]
}
